expect: At least 1 tradition reveals vocabulary for gaps swarm hasn't addressed. Reliabilism's process-reliability tracking predicted as largest gap (L-1390). Building tool_reliability.py should reveal >=3 failure modes invisible to existing tools.
actual: CONFIRMED (n=3, extends L-1390 S510+S514). tool_reliability.py reveals 3 failure modes invisible to existing tools: (1) 53/160 tools (33%) are ISOLATED — zero readers in tool graph, epistemically unjustified per Goldman. (2) 23 tools are WRITE-ONLY — referenced in lessons but no tool reads their output (integration gap). (3) science_quality.py is the only tool with truth_rate <85% (83.3%), meaning lessons referencing it have disproportionate falsification. Existing tools (dogma_finder, validate_beliefs, knowledge_state) capture NONE of these — they operate at belief/lesson level, not tool/process level.
diff: Expected >=3 new failure modes, found exactly 3 distinct classes. Reliabilism gap CONFIRMED as largest (L-1390 prediction holds at n=3). Surprising: only 1 tool below 85% truth rate — tool-level reliability is HIGH (mean R=0.445 dragged down by integration, not truth). The real gap is integration (mean 0.492), not accuracy.
new_failure_modes: ISOLATED: 53 tools with 0 readers — epistemically unjustified (Goldman: no reliable process if no downstream consumer)
WRITE-ONLY: 23 tools referenced in lessons but disconnected from tool graph — L-1101 integration failure at tool level
TRUTH-GAP: science_quality.py 83.3% — lessons citing it have higher falsification rate than baseline
